Valuation: -1.20 diagnostic only — not an allocation input.

The Bitcoin Demand Index tracks the conviction of meaningful BTC holders — long-term on-chain holders, ETF flows, and corporate treasuries — then translates that composite into a simple posture signal inside the broader Milk Road On-chain Dashboard. Stay long while the demand index is above zero; move to cash when it drops below −0.3; hold your current position in between.

This week’s read · Bitcoin Demand Index · 2026-06-16 (cached)

The dashboard is CASH as of June 16, with the Bitcoin Demand Index at -1.02 — well below the 0.00 threshold required to enter a LONG position. Holder Behavior is the sole production input and it tells the same story: on-chain long-term holders are deeply offside at -1.64 and spot ETF flows are weaker still at -1.75, leaving Strategy treasury accumulation (+0.33) as the only cohort pulling in a constructive direction. The seven-day drift is encouraging — the index has recovered +0.20 — but at -1.02 it needs to travel another full point before it clears the entry gate, and the noise band means we hold CASH even as it improves unless and until it crosses 0.00 cleanly. The view flips to LONG if ETF inflows reaccelerate and on-chain holder behavior firms enough to push the composite above zero; a deterioration back through -0.30 is no longer the risk given how far below that level we already sit.

1How the demand index has evolved

Bitcoin Demand Index How the signal works: the Bitcoin Demand Index is the holder-conviction z-score; MROI remains the internal technical handle. Green/amber/red backgrounds show LONG, HOLD, and CASH zones; the hero posture shows the current state.

Bitcoin Demand Index over time; BTC can be toggled as a normalized reference overlay. Green is LONG, amber is HOLD, red is CASH.

Bitcoin Demand Index BTC

2What drives the demand indexDecision inputs

Holder conviction. The Bitcoin Demand Index is driven by three cohorts: on-chain HODL behavior, Strategy treasury accumulation, and spot ETF flows. Click any row to open the input table and raw history behind the current z-score.

Strategy treasury cohort: Strategy is currently the corporate treasury input.

3Reference LibrarySupplementary context

Supplementary context indicators — not part of the decision rule. These valuation lenses help with BTC cycle awareness, but they do not drive the Bitcoin Demand Index posture.

IndicatorCategoryLatestNotes
MVRV-ZValuation—Market value to realized value, z-scored variant. Source: Bitcoin Magazine Pro.
Market value to realized value, z-scored variant. Source: Bitcoin Magazine Pro.
STH MVRVValuation—Short-term holder cost-basis pressure. Source: Bitcoin Magazine Pro.
Short-term holder cost-basis pressure. Source: Bitcoin Magazine Pro.
RHODL RatioValuation—Realized-value age-band valuation oscillator. Source: Bitcoin Magazine Pro.
Realized-value age-band valuation oscillator. Source: Bitcoin Magazine Pro.
Puell MultipleValuation—Daily mining issuance revenue versus its 365-day average. Source: Bitcoin Magazine Pro.
Daily mining issuance revenue versus its 365-day average. Source: Bitcoin Magazine Pro.
Library entries do not drive the headline posture — they are context indicators that explain cycle narratives around the decision rule.
